name: Pasta with Scallops/Vegetables Jjgf65a
recipeIngredient: Fresh white or spinach fettuccine, 10 ounces
Chicken broth or clam juice, 1/2 cup
Red or yellow pepper (seeded and diced), 1
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 2 quarts of salted water to a boil. Add fettuccine and bring to a boil.
2. Add broccoli florets and cook for 3 to 5 minutes, until tender (if using dried pasta, cook the pasta 5 minutes first, then add broccoli and cook an additional 4 to 5 minutes).
3. Drain and toss the pasta with broccoli.
4. Heat olive oil in a large, nonstick skillet over medium heat. Sauté garlic for 1 minute until fragrant.
5. Add peppers and mushrooms; cook, stirring constantly for 3 to 4 minutes, or until peppers begin to soften.
6. Stir in scallops with any accumulated juice; cook just until scallops begin to go opaque.
7. Stir in white wine and broth. Add nutmeg, white pepper, and salt. Bring liquid to a simmer and cook for 2 to 4 minutes more depending on the size of the scallops.
8. In a small dish, blend cornstarch and lemon juice to a smooth paste; stir into the scallop mixture. Cook until the sauce thickens slightly.
9. Turn warm pasta into a serving bowl. Add scallop mixture and toss to coat the pasta. If desired, sprinkle with Parmesan cheese. Serve warm. Makes 4 servings.
